summary:
Submental intubation is used as an emergency airway management technique in patients with severe mandibular fractures causing airway obstruction due to mandibular displacement. This approach allows secure ventilation without attempting direct laryngoscopy through the blocked mouth or nasopharynx.  

Final Answer: A

[SUMMARY HERE]
Submental intubation is recommended in cases of severe mandibular fracture-induced airway compromise caused by mandibular displacement, making it essential when traditional intubation methods fail due to physical blockage.

[SUMMARY HERE] 
It serves as a crucial alternative airway strategy when mandibular fractures prevent routine endotracheal intubation by displacing the jaw and blocking the upper airways.
